article_title: Google just fired a warning shot in the AI subscription price wars | TechCrunch

article_items.title: Google just fired a warning shot in the AI subscription price wars | TechCrunch
article_items.description: Google just made it significantly cheaper to enjoy its budget AI subscription tier.
article_items.excerpt: Google just made it significantly cheaper to enjoy its budget AI subscription tier.
article_items.summary: The company announced Monday that it is cutting the monthly price of Google AI Plus from $7.99 to $4.99 - while doubling the storage included at that tier, from 200 gigabytes to 400 gigabytes. Google AI Plus launched in January as the most affordable paid AI subscription in the U.S. That competition has been building for nearly a year in markets like India, one of the fastest-growing AI user bases in the world. “My prediction for a lot of these infrastructure companies - and when I say infrastructure, I mean an OpenAI or an Anthropic, or the backend components, energy, chips, hosting - there will be a period of time when these companies are valuable,” he said. Vikas Kansal, product lead for Gemini AI subscriptions, said on X that the storage updates would roll out to users over the next several days. It includes a decent feature set , too, including video generation via Omni Flash; the creative studio Google Flow; and NotebookLM, Google’s AI research assistant.
article_items.what_happened: The company announced Monday that it is cutting the monthly price of Google AI Plus from $7.99 to $4.99 - while doubling the storage included at that tier, from 200 gigabytes to 400 gigabytes. Google AI Plus launched in January as the most affordable paid AI subscription in the U.S.

Google AI Plus launched in January as the most affordable paid AI subscription in the U.S.
It includes a decent feature set , too, including video generation via Omni Flash; the creative studio Google Flow; and NotebookLM, Google’s AI research assistant.

WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence, and more Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today Waymo bought Apple’s self-driving car proving ground for.
Vikas Kansal, product lead for Gemini AI subscriptions, said on X that the storage updates would roll out to users over the next several days.

until now - and that shift has serious consequences for the broader market, suggests Chi-Hua Chien, co-founder and managing partner at Goodwater Capital, a consumer-focused venture firm in the Bay Area.
Google followed in December with a sub-$5 AI Plus plan of its own for Indian users.
The historical parallel he reaches for is instructive.
They’ve always known that raw AI capability would eventually become a commodity, and that the real competition would play out at the application and distribution layer.
Subscription pricing hasn’t been a key battleground among AI providers in the U.S.
But the more interesting story here isn’t about Google’s product lineup.
The new pricing makes that positioning even more explicit.

article_items.title: AirPods Pro 4 Leaks Reveal Infrared Cameras, What to Expect Before September - The Gadgeteer
article_items.description: If you were waiting for a sign that Apple is ready to rethink what earbuds can do, 2026's supply-chain reports may be it. Analyst Ming-Chi Kuo is pointing to
article_items.excerpt: If you were waiting for a sign that Apple is ready to rethink what earbuds can do, 2026's supply-chain reports may be it. Analyst Ming-Chi Kuo is pointing to
article_items.summary: The camera modules are expected to be small enough to preserve the current stem design, and some reports suggest Apple may ship the camera-equipped model as a new “AirPods Ultra” rather than a straight Pro 4. The Pro 2 landed in September 2022, got a USB-C revision in September 2023, and the Pro 3 only just launched in September 2025. The idea sounds outlandish until you realize a University of Washington team led by an ex-Apple AirPods engineer already built a working version this spring. If this is a Pro 4, the Pro 3 gets discontinued. Analyst Ming-Chi Kuo is pointing to a September launch for the next AirPods Pro, with Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman tracking the broader chip refresh. If this is a Pro 4, the Pro 3 gets discontinued.

key_points: The camera modules are expected to be small enough to preserve the current stem design, and some reports suggest Apple may ship the camera-equipped model as a new “AirPods Ultra” rather than a straight Pro 4.
The Pro 2 landed in September 2022, got a USB-C revision in September 2023, and the Pro 3 only just launched in September 2025.
If this is a Pro 4, the Pro 3 gets discontinued.
Analyst Ming-Chi Kuo is pointing to a September launch for the next AirPods Pro, with Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman tracking the broader chip refresh.
Either way, the camera model is expected to be the most expensive AirPods Apple has shipped.
The current AirPods Pro 3 retails at $249.
The Upgrades We’re More Certain About The H3 chip is widely expected, following the H2 in the Pro 3 and AirPods 4, bringing better power efficiency and faster on-device processing.
The AirPods Pro 4 or AirPods Ultra , or whatever Apple calls the camera-equipped model, is shaping up to be more than a routine refresh.
Should You Buy AirPods Pro 3 Now or Wait AirPods Pro 3 launched at $249 in September 2025.

Their VueBuds prototype , unveiled in April 2026 and earning an Honorable Mention at CHI ’26, embeds rice-grain-sized cameras in each stem and pipes low-resolution, black-and-white images to a phone, where an on-device.
Kuo has flagged fall 2026 as the window for the camera-equipped model, with Apple’s rumored foldable “iPhone Ultra” event as the likely stage.
MacRumors has outlined two pricing scenarios: if Apple keeps the camera model at $249, it replaces the Pro 3; if it lands around $299 or higher, it slots above the Pro 3 as a new tier.
